note: Generated from Lean proof target tables in docs/book_outline.md. Edit the outline, not this file.

records.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
records.formal_target: A layer without external-action authority can only produce an action through an authorized handoff.

records.chapter_title: ASI Is a Stack, Not a Model
records.formal_target: A handoff that exceeds the caller authority ceiling is rejected.
A route is minimum viable only when no lower-cost authorized route satisfies the required quality predicate.
A routed or compressed result with open obligations cannot be promoted without a residual record.
Every transition preserves or lowers the active authority ceiling unless a governance grant is present.
A missing grant blocks execution rather than becoming default authorization.
A component with a failed required invariant cannot be promoted.
An unbounded authority field is detected as a governance failure.
Support-state transitions require the corresponding evidence artifact.
A claim cannot be promoted when required evidence is absent.

records.chapter_title: Human Intent as a Formal Input
records.formal_target: A compiled intent contract preserves declared constraints and stop conditions.

records.chapter_title: Human Intent as a Formal Input
records.formal_target: A contract missing required authority cannot compile to an executable job.
An admitted plan satisfies every active constitutional predicate.
A self-modification that weakens a protected predicate is rejected.
Protected agency rights remain available after an accepted transition.
A transition that removes a required correction pathway is rejected.
A decision with unresolved protected conflicts carries a residual conflict record.
A high-stakes conflict cannot bypass the required review predicate.

records.chapter_title: Governance Rights: Fork, Exit, and Audit
records.formal_target: A governance transition preserves required audit and exit capabilities.

records.chapter_title: Governance Rights: Fork, Exit, and Audit
records.formal_target: A transition that removes a protected right is rejected or marked invalid.
An implementation can replace another only if it satisfies the field qualification predicate.
A replacement that expands authority without a governance grant is rejected.
A replacement commit requires qualification evidence and rollback metadata.
A failed regression blocks promotion of the replacement.
A secret handle can be substituted only inside an authorized execution boundary.
A context packet with insufficient clearance cannot enter a protected SCIF.
An improvement transition preserves all protected invariants.
A proposal evaluated only by the component being replaced cannot be promoted.
A compiled execution job preserves the parent contract constraints.
An execution job without required approval cannot transition to running.
A valid command contract contains objective, constraints, output contract, verification, and failure behavior.
A hidden or conflicting instruction cannot override an explicit contract constraint.
A plan node inherits the authority ceiling of its parent contract unless governance lowers it.
A plan with unsatisfied required constraints cannot be dispatched.
A dispatchable plan graph is acyclic and all dependencies precede dependents.
A node whose quality predicate fails must escalate or emit a residual.
A compiled artifact preserves all required IR obligations.
A repair that invalidates an existing obligation cannot be accepted without updating the ledger.
A context reference resolves only when the requested address and version are valid for the snapshot.
A mandatory context miss produces a typed fault rather than a best-effort packet.

records.chapter_title: Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates
records.formal_target: A derived context cell carries source bindings and a declared loss/use contract.

records.chapter_title: Semantic Pages, Context Cells, and Certificates
records.formal_target: A summary cannot increase the authority ceiling of its source cells.

records.chapter_title: Context Transactions, Snapshots, Mounts, and Taint
records.formal_target: A snapshot read sees the committed events in its declared view.

records.chapter_title: Context Transactions, Snapshots, Mounts, and Taint
records.formal_target: A tainted source produces tainted derived context unless declassification is authorized.
A context packet admitted for use may still be marked inadequate for a target claim.
A high-risk claim with inadequate context cannot receive a verified support label.
A claim update preserves prior evidence and revision history.
A contradiction prevents promotion until resolved, bounded, or recorded as residual.
A claim at a formal support tier carries a valid proof or justification artifact reference.
A failed verifier result downgrades or blocks the claim rather than promoting it.

records.chapter_title: Unified Adaptive Tribunal and Adversarial Review
records.formal_target: A tribunal verdict includes reviewer roles, evidence references, and unresolved dissent.

records.chapter_title: Unified Adaptive Tribunal and Adversarial Review
records.formal_target: A high-risk artifact cannot be accepted when required tribunal review is absent.
A job transitions only through valid lifecycle states.
A job requiring approval cannot execute before approval is recorded.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay
records.formal_target: Every produced artifact records its parent job and source/context references.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Graphs, Audit Logs, and Replay
records.formal_target: An artifact with missing required provenance cannot support a promoted claim.

records.chapter_title: Runtime Adapters, Tool Permissions, and Human Approval
records.formal_target: A runtime adapter invocation is valid only when job permissions include the adapter capability.

records.chapter_title: Runtime Adapters, Tool Permissions, and Human Approval
records.formal_target: A high-impact adapter call without approval is rejected.

records.chapter_title: Procedural Memory and Cognitive Loop Closure
records.formal_target: A generated tool records its source traces, parameters, and verification result.

records.chapter_title: Procedural Memory and Cognitive Loop Closure
records.formal_target: A tool with failed regression cannot be promoted to routable status.
A router may select only specialists whose authority and readiness satisfy the task requirement.
A failed readiness predicate routes to fallback or residual, not promotion.

records.chapter_title: Readiness Gates, Residual Escrow, and Quarantine
records.formal_target: A module can enter promoted state only after all required gates pass.

records.chapter_title: Readiness Gates, Residual Escrow, and Quarantine
records.formal_target: A quarantined module cannot be selected for ordinary execution routes.
A runtime core promotion requires readiness, regression, and replay evidence references.
A runtime claim sourced only from unavailable text cannot be promoted above argument state.

records.chapter_title: Personal Compute Hives and Federated Edge Intelligence
records.formal_target: A hive scheduler admits a job only when identity, data, tool, federation, and approval policy checks pass before optimization.

records.chapter_title: Personal Compute Hives and Federated Edge Intelligence
records.formal_target: A faster node cannot be selected when its policy membrane forbids the job's data, authority, or network scope.

records.chapter_title: Personal Compute Hives and Federated Edge Intelligence
records.formal_target: A high-risk hive job that requires approval cannot execute unless a bound approval receipt is present.

records.chapter_title: Personal Compute Hives and Federated Edge Intelligence
records.formal_target: External hive access requires an active lease with scope, sandbox, evidence, expiration, and revocation records.

records.chapter_title: Compact Generative Systems and Residual Honesty
records.formal_target: A compact representation with unresolved obligations carries residual records.

records.chapter_title: Compact Generative Systems and Residual Honesty
records.formal_target: A lossy representation cannot be marked exact without verification evidence.
An exact reconstruction claim requires generator output plus repair residual to equal the target.
A failed verification blocks exactness promotion.
A fast generation route records generation mode, verifier, acceptance predicate, risk tier, and fallback before promotion.
Raw tokens per second cannot promote a fast generation claim without accepted or verified token evidence, task-success evidence, and a baseline.
A compressed artifact used for a task must pass that task probe or route to fallback.
A compression record cannot omit residual or fallback metadata.
A semantic node marked grounded has at least one provenance link.
A hierarchy update preserves prior node references or records supersession.
A task budget cannot disable required safety or verification gates.
A high-risk task with insufficient verification budget is blocked or escalated.
A simulation claim includes declared scope, fidelity, and resource bounds.
An experiment result cannot exceed the declared fidelity support of its simulation.
A substrate adoption record includes baseline, measured target, and falsification criterion.
A substrate without passing evidence remains non-core.

records.chapter_title: Circle Calculus and Proof-Carrying AI Contracts
records.formal_target: A proof-carrying AI contract exposes theorem references, deterministic fields, and an explicit non-claim boundary before downstream use.

records.chapter_title: Circle Calculus and Proof-Carrying AI Contracts
records.formal_target: A downstream claim cannot be promoted solely from contract readiness without a workload, baseline, metric, and evidence artifact.

records.chapter_title: Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts
records.formal_target: A cyclic memory claim records residue and winding or marks aliasing as visible residual risk.

records.chapter_title: Coil Attention, Cyclic Memory, and Recurrence Contracts
records.formal_target: Sparse coverage or freshness facts alone cannot promote a retrieval-quality claim.

records.chapter_title: CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, and Cyclic Mixers
records.formal_target: A cyclic mixer claim records the structural invariant separately from quality, runtime, memory, and parameter claims.

records.chapter_title: CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, and Cyclic Mixers
records.formal_target: A cyclic substrate cannot be promoted without ordinary baselines and recorded tradeoff metrics.

records.chapter_title: Executable Specifications and Lean Proof Envelope
records.formal_target: A proof target marked implemented has an existing module and passes the build.

records.chapter_title: Executable Specifications and Lean Proof Envelope
records.formal_target: A proof target for a non-operational claim remains planned or blocked, not implemented.
A capability promotion requires benchmark evidence and preserved regression records.
A saturated benchmark cannot be the sole basis for higher readiness promotion.

records.chapter_title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
records.formal_target: An admitted policy update records target layer, reward signal, update constraint, evaluation refs, governance gates, and rollback plan.

records.chapter_title: Policy Optimization and Learning from Feedback
records.formal_target: A policy update with unverified reward or missing governance gate cannot be promoted.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward-managed work contract records objective, authority, allowed tools, forbidden tools, verification requirements, budget, and non-claims before dispatch.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A steward action that exceeds treasury policy, changes governance rules, or touches protected assets cannot execute without explicit approval evidence.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: A stewarded release publication requires test, evidence, changelog, residual, and approval records.

records.chapter_title: Artifact Steward Agents and Living Project Governance
records.formal_target: When sunset criteria are met, ordinary work generation is blocked until a sunset review is opened.
An end-to-end trace contains required artifacts for each layer handoff.
A trace with a missing governance gate cannot be marked valid.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: An implementation-reference claim names the report, config, or tool surface and does not rely on dashboard prose alone.

records.chapter_title: Project Theseus as Report-First Implementation Reference
records.formal_target: A capability or self-evolution promotion is blocked when required gate reports are absent or failing.
A roadmap phase can unlock a dependent phase only after acceptance gates pass.
A phase milestone cannot promote a claim without evidence artifacts.
Every chapter in the manifest has outline proof targets and generated claim placeholders.
A structural update without regenerated scaffold/proof manifest is invalid.

records.chapter_title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
records.formal_target: A source-derived claim requires a source note or equivalent ingested-source artifact.

records.chapter_title: Open Research Agenda and Bibliography Plan
records.formal_target: A new source cannot be assigned to a non-existent chapter id.
